page.title: St Patrick's Day Yacht Charter Marbella 2027: Best Boats & Routes

data.meta_description: Book a St Patrick's Day yacht charter Marbella 2027 with our fleet of 18 boats from Puerto Banús. Prices from €749/2h, skipper & drinks included. Reserve now.
data.summary: Celebrate St Patrick's Day 2027 on a yacht charter in Marbella. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús with licensed skipper, drinks, and snacks included – from €749 for two hours.
key_facts: St Patrick's Day 2027: Wednesday 17 March – book early for best availability.
Fleet of 18 yachts from Puerto Banús, including Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 from €749/2h.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (beer, wine, cava, soft drinks), and snacks.
Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) comes with free jet ski – minimum 4h at €4,719.
Routes: Marbella coastline, Cabopino, Estepona, or Sotogrande – up to you.
Irish-themed extras available on request: green decorations, Guinness, and live music.
Deposit required to secure date; full payment 14 days before charter.
Contact us via WhatsApp for pricing on boats not listed in Tier A or B.

faq.q: What is the best boat for St Patrick's Day in Marbella?
faq.a: For a group of up to 9, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 (€749/2h) are ideal. For larger parties up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h) includes a free jet ski. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Can I bring my own Guinness or Irish drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own drinks on board. Our standard charter already includes beer, white wine, cava, and soft drinks. If you want Guinness or Irish whiskey, bring it along – no corkage fee.

faq.q: How far in advance should I book for St Patrick's Day 2027?
faq.a: We recommend booking at least 4–6 weeks ahead, especially for popular boats like the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge. March 17th 2027 is a Wednesday, so mid-week availability may be better, but still limited.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for St Patrick's Day charters?
faq.a: Cancellations made 14+ days before the charter receive a full refund. Within 14 days, the deposit is non-refundable. We recommend travel insurance for peace of mind.

faq.q: Can we add Irish-themed decorations or music?
faq.a: Absolutely. We can arrange green bunting, balloons, and a playlist of Irish classics on request. Just let us know when you book, and we’ll coordinate at no extra cost.

faq.q: What time do charters start on St Patrick's Day?
faq.a: Charters depart from Puerto Banús from 10:00 AM onwards. For a sunset cruise, we recommend a 4-hour slot starting at 3:00 PM. The marina is lively all day.

faq.q: Is there a minimum age for passengers?
faq.a: No minimum age, but children must be accompanied by an adult. Life jackets are provided for all sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.

faq.q: Can we dock for lunch at a beach club?
faq.a: Yes. Popular stops include Playa Padre, Nikki Beach, or a quiet cove like Cala del Faro. The skipper will advise on mooring availability. Lunch is at your own cost.

data.body_html:
St Patrick's Day 2027 falls on Wednesday 17 March – and there's no better way to celebrate than with a St Patrick's Day yacht charter Marbella 2027. Whether you're after a lively afternoon with friends or a relaxed family cruise, our fleet of 18 boats departs from Puerto Banús with everything included: skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. Prices start at €749 for two hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. Book early to secure your preferred vessel.

Why Choose a St Patrick's Day Yacht Charter in Marbella?

Marbella in March averages 18°C – warm enough to enjoy the sun on deck. Puerto Banús is the perfect base, with its glamorous marina and easy access to the Costa del Sol coastline. A private yacht means no crowded pubs, no queues, and total flexibility. You can anchor at Cala del Faro, cruise past the Golden Mile, or head to Estepona for lunch. Plus, the Irish community in Marbella is strong – expect a friendly, festive atmosphere on the water.

Our Fleet for St Patrick's Day 2027

We operate a diverse fleet from Puerto Banús. Below are the boats best suited for groups of different sizes. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and IVA (21%).

BoatLengthMax PaxPrice (from)Notes
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h – €2,299/8hGreat value, all-inclusive
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h – €2,299/8hSpacious cockpit, ideal for groups
Mangusta 80 (Grey/White)24m12€4,719/4hIncludes free jet ski
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On requestFlybridge with sun pads
Fairline Targa 12m12m10On requestSporty cruiser

For boats not listed above (e.g., Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m), pricing is on request via WhatsApp. We'll tailor a quote for your group size and budget.

Best Routes for St Patrick's Day

Your skipper can customise the route. Popular options include:

  • Marbella Coastline: Cruise past the Golden Mile, stop at Playa Padre for a swim.
  • Cabopino: Anchor at the dunes – quiet and scenic, 20 minutes from Puerto Banús.
  • Estepona: Head west to the old town marina, about 30 minutes at 20 knots.
  • Sotogrande: A longer cruise (1 hour) past luxury villas and the La Concha mountain backdrop.

All routes include time for swimming, sunbathing, and enjoying the included drinks and snacks.

Irish-Themed Extras

Make your St Patrick's Day charter extra special. We can arrange:

  • Green bunting and balloons for the boat
  • An Irish playlist on the onboard sound system
  • Guinness and Irish whiskey (bring your own – no corkage fee)
  • Traditional Irish snacks (e.g., Tayto crisps) on request

Just let us know when you book, and we'll coordinate the details at no extra cost.

How to Book Your St Patrick's Day Yacht Charter

Booking is straightforward. Contact us via WhatsApp or our online form with your preferred boat, date, and duration. We'll confirm availability and send a deposit invoice (usually 50%). Full payment is due 14 days before departure. For last-minute bookings (within 14 days), full payment is required. We recommend reserving at least 4–6 weeks in advance for St Patrick's Day 2027.

Explore more options on our yacht charter Marbella page.

What's Included in Every Charter

  • Licensed skipper (English and Spanish speaking)
  • Fuel for the full charter duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

No hidden fees. The price you see is the price you pay.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best boat for St Patrick's Day in Marbella?

For a group of up to 9, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 (€749/2h) are ideal. For larger parties up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h) includes a free jet ski. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Can I bring my own Guinness or Irish drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own drinks on board. Our standard charter already includes beer, white wine, cava, and soft drinks. If you want Guinness or Irish whiskey, bring it along – no corkage fee.

How far in advance should I book for St Patrick's Day 2027?

We recommend booking at least 4–6 weeks ahead, especially for popular boats like the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge. March 17th 2027 is a Wednesday, so mid-week availability may be better, but still limited.

What is the cancellation policy for St Patrick's Day charters?

Cancellations made 14+ days before the charter receive a full refund. Within 14 days, the deposit is non-refundable. We recommend travel insurance for peace of mind.

Can we add Irish-themed decorations or music?

Absolutely. We can arrange green bunting, balloons, and a playlist of Irish classics on request. Just let us know when you book, and we’ll coordinate at no extra cost.

What time do charters start on St Patrick's Day?

Charters depart from Puerto Banús from 10:00 AM onwards. For a sunset cruise, we recommend a 4-hour slot starting at 3:00 PM. The marina is lively all day.

Is there a minimum age for passengers?

No minimum age, but children must be accompanied by an adult. Life jackets are provided for all sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.

Can we dock for lunch at a beach club?

Yes. Popular stops include Playa Padre, Nikki Beach, or a quiet cove like Cala del Faro. The skipper will advise on mooring availability. Lunch is at your own cost.
